recommended_actions: Apply EXP-182 patches (P1 part2b 2b.5 stub, P2 part4 scorecard, P3 part4 4.1 full rewrite) to data/sections.json.
Mark EXP-178, EXP-179, EXP-180 in expansion-tracker.json as status='superseded', superseded_by='EXP-182'.
Mark EXP-182 in expansion-tracker.json as status='complete' with output_file='monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-182.json'.
Close ISS-1109 (WIN-073/075/076 disambiguation integrated into new Part 4.1 table Notation paragraph and Prior-WIN column).
Close ISS-1115 (NMP Longitude moved to DISCRIMINATING).
Close ISS-1116 (BORROWED count corrected to 7, non-overlap tally reconciles to 28).
Close ISS-1118 (Bottom-line paragraph rewritten to match table totals).
Close ISS-1119 ('λg is the ruler' reframed as 'most-frequently-shared of several rulers').
summary: EXP-182 is a single large restructure per HNOTE-P4-LIVE-DASHBOARD-RELOCATE-001 that absorbs EXP-178 (Part 4.1 Group B WIN disambiguation), EXP-179 (SEC-2b.5 10→12 knobs recount + 2.8→2.33× inflation factor), and EXP-180 (NMP Longitude LOOSE→DISCRIMINATING, BORROWED tally 6→7) into one coherent rewrite. Part 2b 2b.5 becomes a short pointer-stub (preserving the div id for inbound cross-refs and the capstone landmark role per Steve's directive); Part 4.1 is rewritten to host the relocated 28-domain audit as a table, with the scorecard updated to the 47.69σ / 12 knobs / ~1 discriminator framing, a new statistical-contradiction wedge (47.69σ coexisting with R² = 0.0298 and Pearson r = 0.1727), a Fisher-method independence critique (520 'historical domain p-values' decomposes as 28 × 19 time-series repeats, violating independence both across domains via shared-constant and within-domain via repeated-measurement), and cross-references to the April 17 methodology.json / ai_manifest.json / daily-review pipeline commits. Kernel-of-truth section credits the dome author's rigor improvements and frames the critique as operating within his own stated methodology.
newsworthy: Dome's five-class claim taxonomy applied to only 9 of 69+ claims — rigor improvement is a definition, not yet a broadly applied audit; same panel still shows 47.69σ next to R² = 0.0298.

revision_notes.summary: Updated EXP-182 kernel-of-truth and TLDR to note that the dome author's five-class claim taxonomy (defined in methodology.json) has been applied to only 9 claims in claim_index.json (4 retrospective_structural, 2 pending_contested, 1 supportive_nonunique, 1 prospective_confirmed, 1 open_refinement) as of 2026-04-18. The 69-win registry and the 28-domain live dashboard remain unclassified. The rigor improvement is therefore theoretical at this stage — we cannot evaluate what the taxonomy yields if broadly applied. New falsifiable test for the poller: does the classified count grow toward 69 and 28, or stall near 9?
